name: Herbed Focaccia
recipeInstructions:
A small amount of cornmeal gives this focaccia—a flat bread—its perfect texture for improvised open sandwiches. You might start with thinly sliced salami and cheese.
PREHEAT OVEN TO 450°F. Fifteen minutes before baking the focaccia, put the rack in the center of the oven. If you have two ovens, preheat both; otherwise bake the focaccias in sequence, one after the other. Generously oil two large baking sheets, preferably black steel.
Stir yeast and sugar into warm water. Let stand until foamy, about 5 minutes. Mix flour, cornmeal, and salt in a food processor fitted with a metal blade (a mixer dough hook or by hand). Turn on the processor. Slowly pour the yeast mixture through the feed tube. Also pour 2 tablespoons olive oil through. Process until the dough cleans the inside of the work bowl but is still moist. If it sticks, add a little flour; if it's dry, add a little water. Once the dough is moist but not so wet that it sticks to the sides of the bowl, process until uniformly kneaded, elastic and supple, about 40 seconds.
Transfer the dough to a large plastic food bag, squeeze out air, and seal at the top, allowing room for the dough to expand. Let the dough rise in a warm spot until doubled, about 1 hour. Punch down; use immediately or refrigerate overnight.
Combine remaining 6 tablespoons olive oil, garlic, and dried herbs. Gently heat until warm (not hot) on the stove top or microwave oven.
Divide the dough into half. Let rest for 5 minutes. On a floured board, roll each piece into about a 12-inch round. Transfer to prepared baking sheets. Prick the surface with a fork. Brush with oil mixture, including edges. Sprinkle with kosher salt. Bake, one at a time, until very lightly browned around the edges only, about 8-10 minutes. Do not overbake. Check after 2 minutes in the oven; prick any air pockets with a fork. Brush with any remaining oil, including edges. Sprinkle with fresh herbs and red pepper flakes.
Can be baked in advance and frozen. To do so, cool completely, freeze, and then wrap airtight. Let thaw in wrapping. To reheat, place in a cold oven and set at 300°F. Bake until hot, about 5-7 minutes. Cut into wedges and serve hot. Yield: 2 12-inch rounds.
